posts.hook: Your eye doctor tests 20/20 acuity. But almost no one checks this — and it's the first thing to go.
posts.caption:
Most people pass their vision test with 20/20.

And still struggle to see at night.
Still miss low-contrast text.
Still get headaches from gray backgrounds.

Contrast sensitivity is a completely separate visual skill.

And it's not tested in a standard eye exam.

Free contrast sensitivity test: axeo.vision

posts.hook: Why people with 20/20 vision still crash at night — contrast sensitivity explained.
posts.caption:
Contrast sensitivity = your ability to distinguish objects from similar backgrounds.

Different from acuity (which just tests letter sharpness).

Contrast sensitivity predicts:
- Night driving safety
- Fog navigation
- Reading on screens
- Detecting faces in dim light

It declines earlier than acuity.

The standard Snellen chart can't catch it.

Axeo tests contrast sensitivity in under 2 minutes:
axeo.vision/test

#ContrastSensitivity #NightDriving #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #VisionScience #Optometry

posts.hook: Why night driving gets harder as you age — it's not your glasses.
posts.caption:
As you age, the first thing to decline isn't letter sharpness.

It's contrast sensitivity.

→ Headlights look like halos
→ Lane markings disappear in rain
→ Gray text on white backgrounds strains your eyes

Contrast sensitivity peaks at age 20 and drops steadily.

Most optometrists don't test for it until there's a complaint.

You can test yourself for free in 2 minutes at axeo.vision

#NightDriving #ContrastSensitivity #EyeHealth #Axeo #AgingEyes #VisionTest #EyeCare
posts.visual_notes: Night driving POV without people. Rain-soaked road. Headlight halos. Split-screen: normal vs low contrast sensitivity simulation. Dark, moody, urgent.

posts.hook: You passed your 20/20 test. But do you have good contrast sensitivity? Most people have no idea what this means. A thread:
posts.caption:
1/ Contrast sensitivity is your ability to detect differences between objects and their backgrounds. It's a completely separate visual skill from acuity.

2/ The standard Snellen test (20/20) only measures letter sharpness at high contrast — black letters on a white background. That's the easy version.

3/ Real life is full of low-contrast situations: fog, rain, dim rooms, gray text, faces at dusk. This is where contrast sensitivity matters.

4/ Contrast sensitivity typically peaks around age 20. It declines steadily with age — often before acuity changes are detectable.

5/ Conditions that impair contrast sensitivity first: early cataracts, glaucoma, multiple sclerosis, diabetic retinopathy. It's a sensitive early marker.

6/ The good news: axeo.vision has a free contrast sensitivity test. Takes 90 seconds. No signup.

Share this if you learned something new.
posts.visual_notes: Text thread with optional infographic showing contrast sensitivity curve vs acuity over age. Teal/dark Axeo brand colors.

posts.hook: The vision test that predicts workplace safety — and almost no company tests for it.
posts.caption:
Your annual vision benefits include an acuity test.

But not a contrast sensitivity test.

For employees who:
→ Drive as part of their role
→ Work in low-light environments
→ Operate heavy machinery
→ Work night shifts

...contrast sensitivity is the relevant metric, not 20/20.

Contrast sensitivity predicts:
- Night driving performance
- Reaction time in fog/rain
- Detection of low-contrast hazards
- Screen readability in varied lighting

It declines with age, fatigue, and several health conditions — often silently, well before acuity changes.

A contractor of ours identified two team members with clinically low contrast sensitivity during a routine screening. Neither had failed their standard vision test.

Axeo includes a free contrast sensitivity screening: axeo.vision

#WorkplaceSafety #EmployeeHealth #EyeHealth #ContrastSensitivity #Optometry #HR
